title: podman generate spec
body:
implement a new command `podman generate spec` which can formulate a json specgen to be consumed by both the pod
and container creation API.

supported flags are

--verbose (default true) print output to the terminal
--compact print the json output in a single line format to be piped to the API
--filename put the output in a file
--clone rename the pod/ctr in the spec so it won't conflict w/ an existing entity

Signed-off-by: Charlie Doern <cdoern@redhat.com>

<!--

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

```release-note
introduce podman generate spec which allows users to formulate Specgen data into a json file.
```

comments.body: I think all of the failures are from the API tests hard coding how many containers should exist, I will add a line to remove the one I create and then fix the shell completion

/hold

waiting until @baude and the rest of the team decide specific on this in the coming weeks.
